# x-twitter-scraper_x_tweet (Data Source)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Read-Only

- `author` (Attributes) Author of a tweet with follower count and verification status. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--author))
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `tweet` (Attributes) Full tweet with text, engagement metrics, media, and metadata. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tweet))

<a id="nestedatt--author"></a>
### Nested Schema for `author`

Read-Only:

- `followers` (Number)
- `id` (String)
- `profile_picture` (String)
- `username` (String)
- `verified` (Boolean)


<a id="nestedatt--tweet"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tweet`

Read-Only:

- `bookmark_count` (Number)
- `conversation_id` (String) ID of the root tweet in the conversation thread
- `created_at` (String)
- `entities` (Map of String) Parsed entities from the tweet text (URLs, mentions, hashtags, media)
- `id` (String)
- `is_note_tweet` (Boolean) Whether this is a Note Tweet (long-form post, up to 25,000 characters)
- `is_quote_status` (Boolean) Whether this tweet quotes another tweet
- `is_reply` (Boolean) Whether this tweet is a reply to another tweet
- `like_count` (Number)
- `media` (Attributes List) Attached media items, omitted when the tweet has no media (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tweet--media))
- `quote_count` (Number)
- `quoted_tweet` (Map of String) The quoted tweet object, present when isQuoteStatus is true
- `reply_count` (Number)
- `retweet_count` (Number)
- `source` (String) Client application used to post this tweet
- `text` (String)
- `view_count` (Number)

<a id="nestedatt--tweet--media"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tweet.media`

Read-Only:

- `media_url` (String)
- `type` (String) Available values: "photo", "video", "animated_gif".
- `url` (String)
